rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO APARTMENT ASSOCIATION ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petitioners Motion For Issuance Of Writ With Exhibits A And B
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 5, 2022 line 1. SAN FRANCISCO APARTMENT ASSOCIATION AND SMALL PROPERTY OWNERS OF SAN FRANCISCO INSTITUTE'S NOTICE OF PETITIONERS' MOTION FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT WITH EXHIBITS A AND B is DENIED. Petitioner does not meet its burden of showing Section 37C.4 is invalid on its face due to preemption by state law: (1) the ordinance does not shift the burden of proof, only the burden of production; (2) state law governs the remedies available after a tenant established that a public heath order frustrates the purpose of the lease; and (3) the ordinance does not conflict with executive order N-28-20. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SHU RU LEI VS. XI LING LIN
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 5, 2022 line 9. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. The Complaint is not based on non-payment of rent, and the Notice includes the alleged lawful rent of $1112.11 in compliance with SFRO 37.9(c). Plaintiff need not allege a landlord-tenant relationship between Plaintiff and Defendants Mude Xu and Wen Wen Selena Xu. The court ordered Plaintiff to file a FAC naming Mude Xu and Wen Wen Selena Xu as defendants if they filed a claim of right to possession. As Plaintiff claims a landlord-tenant relationship never existed, Plaintiff need not amend the complaint to allege such a relationship. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
